title: ripgrep bugfix: lowercase size suffixes
prompt: Ripgrep CLI parses human-readable size strings like `2M` through its shared size helper. Extend that behavior so lowercase suffixes `k`, `m`, and `g` work too. Add or update focused tests, keep existing error behavior for invalid input, and verify the relevant tests before finishing.

title: ripgrep feature: terabyte size suffix
prompt: Add terabyte support to ripgrep's human-readable size parser so both `T` and `t` are accepted. Update any docs or help text that mention supported suffixes, add focused tests, and verify the relevant tests before finishing.

title: ripgrep refactor: rename parse_human_readable_size
prompt: Rename the public helper `parse_human_readable_size` to `parse_human_size` throughout ripgrep's CLI crate. Update re-exports, call sites, tests, and docs without changing behavior, and verify the relevant tests before finishing.

title: serde bugfix: lowercase screaming rename-rule aliases
prompt: Serde derive accepts `rename_all` strings like `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE` and `SCREAMING-KEBAB-CASE`. Make the parser accept lowercase spellings of those two existing styles as aliases while preserving canonical behavior. Add focused tests and verify the relevant serde_derive tests before finishing.

title: serde feature: Train-Case rename rule
prompt: Add a new serde derive `rename_all = "Train-Case"` style. For fields it should convert `very_tasty` to `Very-Tasty`; for variants it should convert `VeryTasty` to `Very-Tasty`. Wire it through parsing, rename application, and focused tests, then verify the relevant serde_derive tests before finishing.

title: serde refactor: rename MultiName accessors
prompt: Rename the `MultiName` accessors `serialize_name` and `deserialize_name` to `name_for_serialize` and `name_for_deserialize` across serde_derive. Update all call sites and tests without changing behavior, and verify the relevant serde_derive tests before finishing.
